sources.insurance: Al Jazeera, Caixin Global, CNBC, Insurance Journal (March 3-9 2026)

strait_transits_per_day.notes: 66 commercial transits over 9 days (Feb 28 - Mar 10). Only Iran-linked and China-linked vessels transiting. Mar 8: 2 outbound, 0 inbound. Mar 10: 2 outbound, 0 inbound. Mar 11: ~8-12 transits (slight uptick, mostly shadow fleet).

oil_flow_bbl_per_day.notes: Estimated <10% of normal flow. Only shadow fleet and Iran-linked tankers operating.

lng_flow_bcm_per_year.notes: QatarEnergy halted all LNG production at Ras Laffan after drone attack on Mar 2-3. Effectively zero LNG exports since Mar 3.

war_risk_insurance_pct.notes: New contracts at 1% of hull value per 7-day period (Caixin Global, Mar 7). Pre-crisis: 0.125%. Many insurers cancelled coverage entirely. Premium increases >1000% where available. VLCC charter rates quadrupled to ~$800,000/day.

brent_crude_price_usd.notes: Brent closed at $91.98 on Mar 11 (up 4.76%). Peaked ~$119.50 on Mar 8. IEA historic crude reserve release announced Mar 11 helped ease prices. Pre-crisis ~$63/bbl.

vessels_stranded_count.notes: ~150 ships stranded in region with ~450,000 containers. Maersk, CMA CGM, Hapag-Lloyd suspended all Gulf transits.

vessels_stranded_dwt.notes: Not yet quantified. Estimated significant given 150 vessels including VLCCs.

bypass_pipeline_utilization_pct.notes: Habshan-Fujairah: 1.1M of 1.8M bbl/day capacity. East-West Petroline: 1.4M of 5.0M bbl/day capacity. Combined: 2.5-2.6M of 6.5M theoretical. Pipelines carry crude only — no LNG, petrochemicals, or containerized cargo.

severity_level.label: CRITICAL
severity_level.notes: Level 4 (Critical): <10 vessels/day, only shadow fleet, insurance unavailable, oil peaked >$119, QatarEnergy halted, container lines suspended.
